author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beloved brethren, it ought already to be known to you, as sons of the Church, and well instructed in the school of Christ through all the books of our ancient fathers, who wrote the words of God and the great things of God, that their wish was to consult for our good, who were to live at this period, believers in Christ; who, at a seasonable time came unto us, the first time, in humility; at the second, destined to come in exaltation.…For thus it is said in the Psalms: “Truth shall flourish out of the earth: and righteousness hath looked down from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, therefore, our whole design is, when we hear a Psalm, a Prophet, or the Law, all of which was written before our Lord Jesus Christ came in the flesh, to see Christ there, to understand Christ there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend therefore, beloved, to this Psalm, with me, and let us herein seek Christ; certainly He will appear to those who seek Him, who at first appeared to those who sought Him not; and He will not desert those who long for Him, who redeemed those who neglected Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, the Psalm beginneth concerning Him: of Him it is said:—

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is King, be the people angry” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our Lord Jesus Christ began to reign, began to be preached, after He arose from the dead and ascended into heaven, after He had filled His disciples with the confidence of the Holy Spirit, that they should not fear death, which He had already killed in Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord Christ began then to be preached, that they who wished for salvation might believe in Him; and the peoples who worshipped idols were angry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who worshipped what they had made were angry, because He by whom they were made was declared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He announced, in fact, through His disciples, Himself, who wished them to be converted unto Him by whom they were made, and to be turned away from those things which they had made themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were angry with their Lord in behalf of their idols, they who even if they were angry with their slave on their idol’s account, were to be condemned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For their slave was better than their idol: for God made their slave, the carpenter made their idol.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were so angry in their idol’s behalf, that they feared not to be angry with their Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the words, “be they angry,” are a prediction, not a command; for in a prophecy it is that this is said, “The Lord is King, be the people angry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some good resulteth even from the enraged people: let them be angry, and in their anger let the Martyrs be crowned.…Ye heard when Jeremiah was being read before the reading of the Apostle, if ye listened; ye saw therein the times in which we now live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said, “The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth, let them perish from the earth, and from under the heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, The gods that have not made the heavens and earth, let them perish from the heaven and from the earth; because they never were in heaven: but what did he say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them perish from the earth, and from under the heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if, while the word earth was repeated, the repetition of the word heaven were wanting (because they never were in heaven): he repeateth the earth twice, since it is under heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them perish from the earth, and from under the heaven,” from their temples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider if this be not now taking place; if in a great measure it hath not already happened: for what, or how much, hath remained?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The idols remained rather in the hearts of the pagans, than in the niches of the temples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He who sitteth between the cherubims:” thou dost understand, “He is King: let the earth be stirred up.”…The Cherubim is the seat of God, as the Scripture showeth us, a certain exalted heavenly throne, which we see not; but the Word of God knoweth it, knoweth it as His own seat: and the Word of God and the Spirit of God hath Itself revealed to the servants of God where God sitteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that God doth sit, as doth man; but thou, if thou dost wish that God sit in thee, if thou wilt be good, shalt be the seat of God; for thus is it written, “The soul of the righteous is the seat of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a throne is in our language called a seat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some, conversant with the Hebrew tongue, have interpreted cherubim in the Latin language (for it is a Hebrew term) by the words, fulness of knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, because God surpasseth all knowledge, He is said to sit above the fulness of knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let there be therefore in thee fulness of knowledge, and even thou shalt be the throne of God.…He knoweth all things: for our hairs are numbered before God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the fulness of knowledge which He willed man to know is different from this; the knowledge which He willed thee to have, pertaineth to the law of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who can, thou mayest perhaps say unto me, perfectly know the Law, so that he may have within himself the fulness of the knowledge of the Law, and be able to be the seat of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not disturbed; it is briefly told thee what thou hast, if thou dost wish to have the fulness of knowledge, and to become the throne of God: for the Apostle saith, “Love is the fulfilling of the Law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What followeth then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast lost the whole of thine excuse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ask thine heart; see whether it hath love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there be love there, there is the fulfilment of the Law there also; already God dwelleth in thee, thou hast become the throne of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be the people angry;” what can the angry people do against him who hath become the throne of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou givest heed unto them who rage against thee: Who is it that sitteth within thee, thou givest not heed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art become a heaven, and fearest thou the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Scripture saith in another passage, that the Lord our God doth declare, “The heaven is My throne.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore even thou by having the fulness of knowledge, and by having love, hast been made the throne of God, thou hast become a heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this heaven which we look up to with these eyes of ours, is not very precious before God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Holy souls are the heaven of God; the minds of the Angels, and all the minds of His servants, are the heaven of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is great in Sion, and high above all people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2).…He whom I spoke to thee of as above the Cherubims, is great in Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ask thou now, what is Sion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know Sion to be the city of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The city of Jerusalem is called Sion; and is so called according to a certain interpretation, for that Sion signifieth watching, that is, sight and contemplation; for to watch is to look forward to, or gaze upon, or strain the eyes to see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now every soul is a Sion, if it trieth to see that light which is to be seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if it shall have gazed upon a light of its own, it is darkened; if upon His, it is enlightened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, now that it is clear that Sion is the city of God; what is the city of God, but the Holy Church?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men who love one another, and who love their God who dwelleth in them, constitute a city unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because a city is held together by some law; their very law is Love; and that very Love is God: for openly it is written, “God is Love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore who is full of Love, is full of God; and many, full of love, constitute a city full of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That city of God is called Sion; the Church therefore is Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In it God is great.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye imagine, brethren, that they whose instruments re-echoed yesterday, are not angry with our fastings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let us not be angry with them, but let us fast for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord our God who sitteth in us hath said, He hath Himself commanded us to pray for our enemies, to pray for them that persecute us: and as the Church doth this, the persecutors are almost extinct.…The drunken man doth not offend himself, but he offendeth the sober man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Show me a man who is at last happy in God, liveth gravely, sigheth for that everlasting peace which God hath promised him; and see that when he hath seen a man dancing to an instrument, he is more grieved for his madness, than for a man who is in a frenzy from a fever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then we know their evils, considering that we also have been freed from those very evils, let us grieve for them; and if we grieve for them, let us pray for them; and that we may be heard, let us fast for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we do not keep our own fasts in their holidays.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Different are the fasts which we celebrate through the days of the approaching Passover, through different seasons which are fixed for us in Christ: but through their holidays we fast for this reason, that when they are rejoicing, we may groan for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by their joy they excite our grief, and cause us to remember how wretched they are as yet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since we see many freed thence, where we also have been, we ought not to despair even of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if they are still enraged, let us pray; and if still a particle of earth that hath remained behind be stirred up against us, let us continue in lamentation for them, that to them also God may grant understanding, and that with us they may hear those words, in which we are at this moment rejoicing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these very people, over whom Thou art great in Sion, “Let them confess unto Thy Name, which is great” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy Name was little when they were enraged: it hath become great; let them now confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what sense do we say, that the Name of Christ was little, before it was spread abroad to so great an extent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because His report is meant by His Name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Name was small; already it hath become great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What nation is there that hath not heard of the Name of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore let now the people confess unto Thy Name, which is great, who before were enraged with Thy little Name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore shall they confess?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is “wonderful and holy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy very Name is wonderful and holy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is so preached as crucified, so preached as humbled, so preached as judged, that He may come exalted, that He may come living, that He may come to judge in power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He spareth at present the people who blaspheme Him, because “the long-suffering of God leadeth to repentance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He who now spareth, will not always spare: nor will He, who is now being preached that He may be feared, fail to come to judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will come, my brethren, He will come: let us fear Him, and let us live so that we may be found on His right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He will come, and will judge, so as to place some on the left hand, some on the right.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He doth not act in an uncertain manner, so as to err perchance betwixt men, so that he who should be set on the right hand, be set on the left; or that he who ought to stand on the left, by a mistake of God should stand on the right: He cannot err, so as to place the evil where He ought to set the good; nor to place the good, where He should have set the evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He cannot err, we err, if we fear not; but if we have feared in this life, we shall not then have what to fear for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the King’s honour loveth judgment.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast prepared equity; Thou hast wrought judgment and righteousness in Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we too ought to have judgment, we ought to have righteousness; but He worketh in us judgment and righteousness, who created us in whom He might work them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How ought we too to have judgment and righteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast judgment, when thou dost distinguish evil from good: and righteousness when thou followest the good, and turnest aside from the evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By distinguishing them, thou hast judgment; by doing, thou hast righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Eschew evil,” he saith, “and do good; seek peace, and ensue it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shouldest first have judgment, then righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What judgment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thou mayest first judge what is evil, and what is good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what righteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thou mayest shun evil, and do good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this thou wilt not gain from thyself; see what he hath said, “Thou hast wrought judgment and righteousness in Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O magnify the Lord our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Magnify Him truly, magnify Him well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us praise Him, let us magnify Him who hath wrought the very righteousness which we have; who wrought it in us, Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who but He who justified us, wrought righteousness in us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of Christ it is said, “who justifieth the ungodly.”…“And fall down before His footstool: for He is holy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are we to fall down before?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His footstool.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is under the feet is called a footstool, in Greek ὑποπόδιον, in Latin Scabellum or Suppedaneum.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But consider, brethren, what he commandeth us to fall down before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In another passage of the Scriptures it is said, “The heaven is My throne, and the earth is My footstool.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth he then bid us worship the earth, since in another passage it is said, that it is God’s footstool?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then shall we worship the earth, when the Scripture saith openly, “Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet here it saith, “fall down before His footstool:” and, explaining to us what His footstool is, it saith, “The earth is My footstool.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am in doubt; I fear to worship the earth, lest He who made the heaven and the earth condemn me; again, I fear not to worship the footstool of my Lord, because the Psalm biddeth me, “fall down before His footstool.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ask, what is His footstool? and the Scripture telleth me, “the earth is My footstool.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In hesitation I turn unto Christ, since I am herein seeking Himself: and I discover how the earth may be worshipped without impiety, how His footstool may be worshipped without impiety.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He took upon Him earth from earth; because flesh is from earth, and He received flesh from the flesh of Mary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because He walked here in very flesh, and gave that very flesh to us to eat for our salvation; and no one eateth that flesh, unless he hath first worshipped: we have found out in what sense such a footstool of our Lord’s may be worshipped, and not only that we sin not in worshipping it, but that we sin in not worshipping.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But doth the flesh give life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord Himself, when He was speaking in praise of this same earth, said, “It is the Spirit that quickeneth, the flesh profiteth nothing.”…But when our Lord praised it, He was speaking of His own flesh, and He had said, “Except a man eat My flesh, he shall have no life in him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some disciples of His, about seventy, were offended, and said, “This is an hard saying, who can hear it?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they went back, and walked no more with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It seemed unto them hard that He said, “Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of Man, ye have no life in you:” they received it foolishly, they thought of it carnally, and imagined that the Lord would cut off parts from His body, and give unto them; and they said, “This is a hard saying.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was they who were hard, not the saying; for unless they had been hard, and not meek, they would have said unto themselves, He saith not this without reason, but there must be some latent mystery herein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They would have remained with Him, softened, not hard: and would have learnt that from Him which they who remained, when the others departed, learnt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when twelve disciples had remained with Him, on their departure, these remaining followers suggested to Him, as if in grief for the death of the former, that they were offended by His words, and turned back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He instructed them, and saith unto them, “It is the Spirit that quickeneth, but the flesh profiteth nothing; the words that I have spoken unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand spiritually what I have said; ye are not to eat this body which ye see; nor to drink that blood which they who will crucify Me shall pour forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have commended unto you a certain mystery; spiritually understood, it will quicken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although it is needful that this be visibly celebrated, yet it must be spiritually understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Moses and Aaron among His priests, and Samuel among such as call upon His Name: these called upon the Lord, and He heard them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He spake unto them out of the cloudy pillar” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7).…Of Moses it is not there stated that he was a priest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if he was not this, what was he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Could he be anything greater than a priest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm declareth that he also was himself a priest: “Moses and Aaron among His priests.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They therefore were the Lord’s priests.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Samuel is read of later in the Book of Kings: this Samuel is in David’s times; for he anointed the holy David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Samuel from his infancy grew up in the temple.…He mentioneth these: and by these desireth us to understand all the saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet why hath he here named those?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because we said that we ought here to understand Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, holy brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said above, “O magnify the Lord our God: and fall down before His footstool, for He is holy:” praising some one, that is, our Lord Jesus Christ; whose footstool is to be worshipped, because He assumed flesh, in which He was to appear before the human race; and wishing to show unto us that the ancient fathers also had preached of Him, because our Lord Jesus Christ is Himself the True Priest, he mentioned these, because God spake unto them out of the cloudy pillar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “out of the cloudy pillar”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was speaking figuratively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He spoke in some cloud, those obscure words predicted some one unknown, yet to be manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This unknown one is no longer unknown; for He is known by us, our Lord Jesus Christ.…He who first spoke out of the cloudy pillar, hath in Person spoken unto us in His footstool; that is, on earth, when He had assumed the flesh, for which reason we worship His footstool, for He is holy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He Himself used to speak out of the cloud, which was not then understood: He hath spoken in His own footstool, and the words of His cloud have been understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They kept His testimonies, and the law that He gave them.”…“Thou heardest them,” he saith, “O Lord our God: Thou wast forgiving to them, O God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is not said to be forgiving toward anything but sins: when He pardoneth sins, then He forgiveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what had He in them to punish, so that He was forgiving in pardoning them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was forgiving in pardoning their sins, He was also forgiving in punishing them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And punishedst all their own affections.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even in punishing them Thou wast forgiving toward them: for not in remitting, but also in punishing their sins, hast Thou been forgiving.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider, my brethren, what he hath taught us here: attend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is angry with him whom, when he sinneth, He scourgeth not: for unto him to whom He is truly forgiving, He not only remitteth sins, that they may not injure him in a future life; but also chasteneth him, that he delight not in continual sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Come, my brethren; if we ask how these were punished, the Lord will aid me to tell you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us consider these three persons, Moses, Aaron, and Samuel: and how they were punished, since he said, “Thou hast punished all their own affections:” meaning those affections of theirs, which the Lord knew in their hearts, which men knew not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were living in the midst of the people of God, without complaint from man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what do we say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That perhaps the early life of Moses was sinful; for he fled from Egypt, after slaying a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The early life of Aaron also was such as would displease God; for he allowed a maddened and infatuated people to make an idol to worship; and an idol was made for God’s people to worship.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sin did Samuel, who was given up when an infant to the temple?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He passed all his life amid the holy sacraments of God: from childhood the servant of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing was ever said of Samuel, nothing by men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps God knew of somewhat there to chasten; since even what seemeth perfect unto men, unto that Perfection is still imperfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Artists show many of their works to the unskilful; and when the unskilful have pronounced them perfect, the artists polish them still further, as they know what is still wanting to them, so that men wonder at things they had imagined already perfect having received so much additional polish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This happeneth in buildings, and in paintings, and in embroidery, and almost in every species of art.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first they judge it to be already in a manner perfect, so that their eyes desire nothing further: but the judgment of the inexperienced eye is one, and that of the rule of art another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus also these Saints were living before the eyes of God, as if faultless, as if perfect, as if Angels: but He who punished all their own affections, knew what was wanting in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He punished them not in anger, but in mercy: He punished them that He might perfect what He had begun, not to condemn what He had cast away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God therefore punished all their affections.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did He punish Samuel? where is this punishment?…What was said unto Moses was a type, not a punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What punishment is death to an old man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What punishment was it, not to enter into that land, into which unworthy men entered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is said of Aaron?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He also died an old man: his sons succeeded him in the priesthood: his son afterwards ruled in the priesthood: how did He punish Aaron also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Samuel also died a holy old man, leaving his sons as his successors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I seek for the punishment inflicted upon them, and according to men I find it not: but according to what I know the servants of God suffer every day, they were day by day punished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Read ye, and see the punishments, and ye also who are advanced bear the punishments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every day they suffered from the obstinate people, every day they suffered from the ungodly livers; and were compelled to live among those whose lives they daily censured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was their punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He unto whom it is small hath not advanced far; for the ungodliness of others tormenteth thee in proportion as thou hast departed far from thine own.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O magnify the Lord our God!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again we magnify Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who is merciful even when He striketh, how is He to be praised, how is He to be magnified?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Canst thou show this unto thy son, and cannot God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou art not good when thou dost caress thy son, and evil when thou strikest him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both when thou dost caress him thou art a father, and when thou strikest him, thou art his father: thou dost caress him, that he may not faint; thou strikest him, that he may not perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O magnify the Lord our God, and worship Him upon His holy hill: for the Lord our God is holy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As he said above, “O magnify the Lord our God and fall down before His footstool:” now we have understood what it is to worship His footstool: thus also but now after he had magnified the Lord our God, that no man might magnify Him apart from His hill, he hath also praised His hill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is His hill?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We read elsewhere concerning this hill, that a stone was cut from the hill without hands, and shattered all the kingdoms of the earth, and the stone itself increased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the vision of Daniel which I am relating.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This stone which was cut from the hill without hands increased, and “became,” he saith, “a great mountain, and filled the whole face of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us worship on that great mountain, if we desire to be heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Heretics do not worship on that mountain, because it hath filled the whole earth; they have stuck fast on part of it, and have lost the whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they acknowledge the Catholic Church, they will worship on this hill with us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we already see how that stone that was cut from the mountain without hands hath increased, and how great tracts of earth it hath prevailed over, and unto what nations it hath extended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the mountain whence the stone was hewn without hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jewish kingdom, in the first place; since they worshipped one God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence was hewn the stone, our Lord Jesus Christ.…That stone then was born of the mountain without hands: it increased, and by its increase broke all the kingdoms of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath become a great mountain, and hath filled the whole face of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the Catholic Church, in whose communion rejoice that ye are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who are not in her communion, since they worship and praise God apart from this same mountain, are not heard unto eternal life; although they may be heard unto certain temporal things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not flatter themselves, because God heareth them in some things: for He heareth Pagans also in some things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not the Pagans cry unto God, and it raineth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He maketh His sun to rise over the good and the bad, and sendeth rain upon the just and the unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Boast not therefore, Pagan, that when thou criest unto God, God sendeth rain, for He sendeth rain upon the just and the unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath heard thee in temporal things: He heareth thee not in things eternal, unless thou hast worshipped in His holy hill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Worship Him upon His holy hill: for the Lord our God is holy.”…
